housing.summary: USC housing is reportedly competitive and can fill up fast, with students worried about securing beds. One student mentioned USC 'ran out of beds last year,' suggesting demand exceeds supply. Out-of-state transfers have noted being excluded from dorms as a significant drawback.
key_quotes: USC housing is pretty competitive; they ran out of beds last year and me and my friends are trying to get ahead on that.
Transfers weren't allowed to dorm, and it's overall [a problem].
Tips for scheduling classes? Going to Orientation at USC this week.

social_life.summary: USC has a reputation as a party school with a wealthy, socially active student body, sometimes mockingly called 'University of Spoiled Children.' Students who struggle socially report feeling isolated, and at least one student transferred out after making very few friends. The football culture and Greek life appear dominant in the social scene.
key_quotes: Intended Major(s): Double major in partying and going to football games.
I met very few 'friends' and overall I was just extremely unhappy. As a result, I submitted multiple transfer applications.
It's called University of Spoiled Children for a reason. A lot of people get in with connections.

dining.summary: There is virtually no student discussion about USC dining in these posts, suggesting it is neither a standout positive nor a major complaint driving online conversation. No specific dining hall experiences, food quality comments, or meal plan issues were mentioned across the 247 posts.
key_quotes: No meaningful dining-specific quotes were found in the dataset.
No meaningful dining-specific quotes were found in the dataset.
No meaningful dining-specific quotes were found in the dataset.

mental_health.summary: Multiple students describe serious mental health struggles at USC, including depression, isolation, and complete academic disengagement. At least one student described getting 'super depressed the first few months' and isolating entirely from peers. The pressure of the school's high costs and competitive environment appears to compound mental health challenges.
key_quotes: I got super depressed the first few months here and completely isolated myself and gave up on making friends.
I cried, this is all so overwhelming... I've been so low and hopeless lately.
Ole boi gonna have to bury his aspirations of being at USC cuz I suck ass.

financial_aid.summary: USC's financial aid is a major, recurring pain point. The sticker price has exceeded $90,000/year, and many students report receiving little to no aid from the middle class. However, USC did announce free tuition for families earning under $80,000, which generated significant positive attention. Students frequently describe the financial aid office as inflexible.
key_quotes: The financial aid office is literally about to be flooded with how many shit offers they've given to middle class families.
USC only gave me 17,000 in loans, so I would have to pay almost 60,000 tuition, which is hella expensive.
They are eliminating using home equity in calculations too [as part of the new $80k free tuition announcement].

academics.summary: USC is generally respected academically, particularly for film, business (Marshall), communications (Annenberg), engineering, and computer science. However, some students question whether the academic quality justifies the extreme cost compared to UC schools. A student pursuing data science contemplated transferring to a UC for better career outcomes despite already being at USC.
key_quotes: For my new career aspirations in data science, I will most likely have to get a masters degree... I am now contemplating going back to community college for a year in order to transfer to a UC school.
USC Annenberg [communications] is rather well known at my school and I was wondering if that would add value to a comm degree post grad.
USC is a highly ranked school [for CS] — Better CS program by a huge amount if we're going off of rank [vs UC Davis].

overall_vibe.summary: USC projects an image of wealth, prestige, and Southern California glamour, but the reality for many students is financial stress, social difficulty, and a campus culture heavily shaped by wealthy students. The school has strong brand recognition and alumni networks, but the gap between the glossy reputation and the actual experience is a recurring theme in student posts.
key_quotes: I go to a T25 = ur def at USC/NYU.
I worked extremely hard my entire life to get into schools with low acceptance rates... Once I finally got to USC, it was virtually a nightmare from day one.
USC is coming out today, but if you don't get in, that is okay! They are really shady.

red_flags.summary: USC has been involved in several major scandals: the Operation Varsity Blues admissions bribery case, an $852 million–$1.1 billion lawsuit involving a gynecologist who sexually assaulted over 700 students, and a Dr. Dre $70 million donation coinciding with his daughter's admission. These issues have permanently damaged trust in the institution for many prospective students.
key_quotes: USC recently paid an 852 million dollar lawsuit to 710 women because of a gynecologist that was a serial offender. SEVEN HUNDRED AND TEN.
I already didn't really like it considering its well-to-do student populace and its crappy financial aid... Varsity Blues and the whole scandal surrounding it really ruined USC for me permanently.
Maybe if USC wasn't paying billions of dollars in lawsuits and scandals and AirPods they could actually give us financial aid.

hidden_gems.summary: USC offers personalized Kindles to scholarship recipients, signaling strong investment in yield for top admitted students. The school's location in LA provides unmatched access to entertainment, tech, and aerospace industry internships, especially through programs like the rocket propulsion lab. USC also announced genuinely generous financial aid reform, eliminating home equity from calculations and offering free tuition under $80k income.
key_quotes: USC (scholarship) — a personalized Kindle!? That yield rate is going up.
USC's rocket propulsion lab (where they build and launch rockets), and many students are able to get internships at tech companies (space especially) considering the location of the school (L.A.).
They are eliminating using home equity in calculations too [as part of the $80k free tuition program].
